title: Vomiting / NG Suction-Induced Metabolic Alkalosis
buzzwords: Intoxicated patient with repeated emesis
Volume depletion with dizziness and dry mucous membranes
Loss of gastric acid raises serum bicarbonate
Hypokalemic hypochloremic alkalosis with paradoxical aciduria
Urine chloride less than 10 mEq/L
Corrected by normal saline infusion
Bulimic patient or chronic NG tube drainage
Parietal cell HCl loss regenerates new HCO3-

title: Diuretic-Induced Metabolic Alkalosis
buzzwords: Edematous patient on chronic outpatient therapy
Contraction alkalosis with hypokalemia
RAAS activation drives distal H+ secretion
Chloride-responsive but urine chloride paradoxically high
Resolves after offending agent is discontinued
Loop or thiazide agent causes NaCl wasting
Furosemide or hydrochlorothiazide use
Saline plus potassium repletion restores acid-base balance

title: Hyperaldosteronism-Induced Metabolic Alkalosis
buzzwords: Resistant hypertension with unexplained hypokalemia
Alkalosis that does not improve with saline
Urine chloride greater than 20 mEq/L
Mineralocorticoid excess drives H+ and K+ wasting
Suppressed renin with elevated aldosterone
Adrenal adenoma or bilateral adrenal hyperplasia
Conn syndrome with chloride-resistant alkalosis
Treated with spironolactone or eplerenone

title: Congenital Tubulopathy-Induced Metabolic Alkalosis
buzzwords: Young patient with persistent hypokalemia and alkalosis
Normotensive salt-wasting despite high urine chloride
Mimics chronic loop or thiazide diuretic use
Family history of unexplained electrolyte abnormalities
Chloride-resistant alkalosis without volume depletion
Inherited defect of distal tubular sodium handling
Bartter, Gitelman, or Liddle syndrome
Treated with potassium and amiloride or spironolactone
